sections.concept: Same as Time & Work but Outlet does negative work.
sections.formula: Net Rate = Sum of Inlets - Sum of Outlets
sections.solvingTechnique: Use positive sign for filling pipes and negative for emptying.

examples.question: Pipe A fills in 10h. Pipe B empties in 5h. (Impossible, but assuming B is slower) Pipe A fill 10h, B fills 20h.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Both filling.
Step 2: 1/10 + 1/20 = 3/20.
Step 3: Time = 20/3 hours.

sections.concept: Leak acts as an outlet pipe.
sections.formula: Time to empty = Capacity / Leak Rate
sections.solvingTechnique: Treat leak as a pipe with negative efficiency.

examples.question: Tank fills in 4h, but leak takes it 5h. Time for leak to empty full tank?
examples.solution:
Step 1: Fill Rate = 1/4. Net Rate = 1/5.
Step 2: Leak = Fill - Net = 1/4 - 1/5 = 1/20.
Step 3: Time = 20 hours.

shortcuts: If A fills in x & B empties in y (y>x), full tank empties in xy/(x-y)?? No fills in xy/(y-x).
commonMistakes: Confusing 'Time to fill with leak' vs 'Time for leak to empty'.
placementTips: Pipes & Cisterns is just Time & Work with negative numbers.
Always check if the tank is being filled or emptied in the question.
